claim: Multiple prompt version stacks (separate folders for Claude vs GPT optimized prompts with nightly sync review) prevent drift and maintain model-specific best practices automatically.
actionable: Create dual prompt folders: root MD files optimized for Claude (avoid all caps), separate folder for GPT 5.2 (use all caps freely). Run nightly cron to sync operational facts and flag drift.
source_title: I've spent 5 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw...

claim: Opus 4.6 does not respond well to all-caps emphasis (unlike GPT); Claude's best practices emphasize natural language and clear instructions without shouting.
actionable: When prompting Opus 4.6, avoid ALL CAPS and aggressive formatting; use natural language instructions and trust the model's instruction-following.
source_title: I've spent 5 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw...

claim: Email sponsorship pipeline with OpenClaw: deterministic sanitizer + frontier scan quarantine + rubric-based scoring + Gmail labels + HubSpot sync reduces manual sponsorship triage from hours to minutes.
actionable: Set up email sponsorship agent with three-layer security (sanitizer, frontier scan, rule-based scoring). Use scores (low/medium/high/exceptional) to auto-reply or escalate to team.
source_title: I've spent 5 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw...

claim: Three-layer prompt injection defense (deterministic sanitizer + frontier scanner in sandbox + elevated risk markers) is necessary for any OpenClaw input ingesting data from the internet.
actionable: Never allow OpenClaw to directly process email, web scrapes, or user input without: 1) deterministic code sanitizing for injection patterns, 2) frontier model scanning in isolated sandbox, 3) risk scoring.
source_title: I've spent 5 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw...

claim: File-based organization in OpenClaw (agents.md, soul.md, user.md, tools.md, memory.md, PRD, etc.) prevents prompt drift and ensures single source of truth for each piece of information.
actionable: Create standardized MD files: agents.md (rules/security), soul.md (identity, max 10 lines), user.md (about you), tools.md (env vars), memory.md (private), PRD (feature index). Never duplicate info.
source_title: I've spent 5 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw...

claim: Scheduling heavy cron jobs overnight (1am Instagram analytics, 1:15am X analytics, 2am CRM) preserves daily token quota and prevents bill shock from asynchronous work during peak hours.

claim: OpenClaw's default memory system (memory.md updated daily) works well when combined with Telegram group topics (limiting context per topic) and nightly pruning of duplicate info in prompt files.
actionable: Keep memory simple; reduce context by using Telegram topics for separation. Run nightly automated pruning of duplicate information across prompt files (~10% reduction every 2 days).
